content.claim: Ethnic and social segregation in Hungary shows a school-level correlation of 0.79 between disadvantage share and Roma share, with approximately one-third of Roma students in highly segregated environments (defined as >25-30% Roma share) despite repeated marginalization-reduction efforts.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Specific threshold definitions (25-30% vs. 50% for 'severe' segregation) vary in literature and may not reflect international consensus
Causality between the tracking system and segregation patterns is inferred but not experimentally established
Current year of data not specified in research notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
